opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Whenever I walk up the front steps, my chest feels like it's in a vice, and the pain shoots straight up into my jaw.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been happening on and off for a couple of days and it's starting to come on with less and less effort — it's scaring me because I live on my own.

symptom_narrative.duration: comes and goes over the last 48 hours; each episode lasts about 5 to 10 minutes
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: quite severe when it comes on, enough to make me stop what I'm doing
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: in the middle of my chest, behind the breastbone, and it shoots up into my jaw and down my left arm
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: brought on by effort — walking up the front steps or carrying the laundry; now it comes on with less effort than a couple of days ago
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting down and resting — it settles within about 5 to 10 minutes
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any exertion, especially going up stairs or carrying things
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel a bit sick in the stomach with it, and a little short of breath
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've been avoiding doing much because I'm frightened it will bring the pain on

past_medical_history.how_managed: I'm supposed to take a cholesterol tablet every day
